## [00:00 - 03:01]

This is David Bass with 3x formulas and I'm going to be showing you some of the configuration items that John was showing you, but this is going to be within a PC environment. So I'm going to be showing you how to work with Camtasia and PowerPoint. This first video is going to show you how to combine a PowerPoint video with audio at the same time. If you are interested in doing it separately, recording audio first and then adding in the PowerPoint, I'll show you that in a second presentation. But in this presentation, we're going to start off in PowerPoint, which might not sound intuitive because we're going to be using Camtasia, but we are going to be starting off in PowerPoint just so you know the version. This is part of the 2010 Office Professional and this is the version. So within PowerPoint, when you installed Camtasia, it should have asked you if you wanted to do a PowerPoint add on or plug in. Here it's called add ins. We'll show it right there. And if it's not already installed, you could probably go into the options menu within Camtasia or you can consult their help menu to see how to add the PowerPoint add in. So you click on the add in button, but what happens is you see this little box right here that has cool things like record and then different things that you could include in the recording. So the default should be the audio and it will obviously record the PowerPoint itself. And when you click the record button, what's going to happen is it's going to go to presentation mode. It should give you a little box in the right hand corner to hit record. And then in the bottom right hand corner, you're going to see a box that gives you the microphone level. And then when you click to begin recording, it will record the entire PowerPoint and anything you record in the audio. And it should give you some options for pausing and stopping. There you go. You have a little F1 to pause and then F11 or escape to stop. And these are configurable, but these are default. So I'm going to try to record it and see what happens. I'm not sure, but click the record button. And you essentially are recording the PowerPoint. I thought it should give you a countdown. And this particular PowerPoint starts off with a video that counts down. Bring it down a little bit so you can see it. Try to get it squared off. And then you record this and you'll be like, hey, this is my presentation. There you go. This is a presentation that I was doing on stage for email. And I kind of just took it through. You can probably only see part of this right now, but so you finger through your presentation. You can use down arrow. You should be able to use the mouse click as well. And you just talk over your slides, hit the next slide when you're ready. And then when you're finished, you hit escape. You should be able to see this. You hit the stop recording button.

## [03:01 - 06:07]

It'll give you a chance to save it and you would save it. And then when you're done saving it, and call this test. And that's fine. When you're done saving it, it should ask what you want to do. You could edit your recording. Hit edit. Should open up Cantasia automatically, almost like magic. It'll already put it in the timeline. You're going to put whatever dimension you have here. So we're going to do 640 by 480 and you hit OK. And then it's going to end up in your timeline down here automatically. You can use these buttons right here, the plus button to maximize your timeline. So let's say in the beginning part here, this is extraneous. In Cantasia, the way that you edit is you have this little bar here. It's a little different than in ScreenFlow. You see this green in the red, green left, red right? Click on the green and you would drag over. And I think you can probably hit Control-X. You can also right mouse button click and just hit cut. And voila, it's gone. So it would start where you wanted to. And essentially you have your entire presentation in this screen area or in this edit area. You can cut the way I just showed you. You have these little buttons right here. So audio right here, you have some cool features up here where you can highlight the audio in the bottom and you can enable noise removal. If for any reason it's too low or too high, you can actually adjust these right here. That's some advanced features that you can mess around with and see if it sounds better or not depending on how your recording worked. And when you're already and you're finished and you're happy with your final product, and again you can also add some things here like transitions, you can do some call outs where you add some cool pieces. And you're already and happy in Camtasia you go to produce and share and produce and share. Here you're going to adjust to custom production settings and you hit next. Should default to MP4, FLVSFW, this is the one you want to check. And here depending on if you want to create an iPod file or any specific files you can check these and you hit next.

## [06:07 - 09:09]

This is where the options look a lot different than they did in ScreenFlow as far as how you get access to them. The first place we're going to go is Flash Options. Here we're going to see three different options in the format. We want MP4. We want to make sure under frame rate we have it set to 15 and I'm starting off with the default so you can kind of see what gets changed. Under key frame I think we can leave that at the default. John has it set to automatic encoding mode quality. You want to switch it to bit rate and then we want to restrict to 1100 kilobits and then under audio we want to make sure it is at bit rate of 96 to 109 is what we want. So I think we can put 96 there. Table of contents we're not doing anything with. Controller, this is just personal preferences. So that's what we can configure there. Let me go and hit apply. Then we hit OK. Under dimensions the video is there. You can do some stuff in here but as you can see I have 640 by 480. That's the fit in size so that's what we want there. Change dimensions. If we go down here we have the fit in 640 by 480. Then we can hit OK which is on the right hand side. Then we hit next. Then under video options this is just basic information. Under HTML options, basic information. Go to next. You don't want to do anything in this page just hit next. Then you name it something that is appropriate to the BSL that you're creating. So call this BSL example. Make sure you know where you're putting it. I've seen people lose files because they don't know where they're putting it. So I'll call it put something in 3x video. Save. Then you hit finish.
